description: LNA Tm validation hardened with a real independent MELTING 5 cross-check (rmelting 1.26.0, MELTING Java engine, method.locked=mct04/owc11). v1.28.0 could only validate against 12 embedded McTigue experimental duplexes because Java was unavailable; v1.29.0 adds (a) the MELTING cross-check on those 12 and (b) an expanded 96-oligo panel spanning length 8-22 and GC 30-72% cross-checked against MELTING as an independent reference.

vs_experiment_mctigue12.note: Primary validation against real absorbance-melting measurements (McTigue 2004, 5 µM, 1 M Na+).

melting_crosscheck_12.matched_concentration_note: MELTING Ct=5.5uM reproduces OligoForge plain-core Tm (51.15 vs 51.10)
melting_crosscheck_12.interpretation: At matched concentration, OligoForge (RMSE 1.86, bias −0.52) and MELTING's independent implementation of the same McTigue parameters (RMSE 2.10, bias −0.09) are both close to experiment — neither dominates. The LNA INCREMENT (locked − core Tm) agrees to mean |Δ| 0.03 °C, max 0.08 °C, proving OligoForge correctly implements the McTigue parameter set.

expanded_panel_96.span: length 8-22, GC 30-72%, all 4 locked bases, internal positions
expanded_panel_96.interpretation: Across 96 diverse oligos, OligoForge's LNA increment matches MELTING's to RMSE 0.075 °C (max 0.31 °C), uniform across locked-base identity (AL/CL/GL/TL) and length. Full-Tm RMSE 1.17 °C reflects the DNA-backbone salt/concentration convention, not the LNA layer.

headline: The LNA layer is now independently validated. Against McTigue's real experimental data the model gives RMSE 1.86 °C / bias −0.52 °C (8/12 within ±2, 12/12 within ±3). An independent MELTING 5 engine confirms the implementation: LNA increments agree to 0.03 °C on the 12 experimental duplexes and 0.075 °C RMSE across an expanded 96-oligo panel. OligoForge and MELTING are an honest tie against experiment (RMSE 1.86 vs 2.10); OligoForge does not claim superiority.
